notShipped.status: All marketing assets remain armed. Evening window (6-9pm) still available for 1 more hour.
notShipped.recommendation: If Tom surfaces in next hour → instant deployment. Otherwise pivot to Monday.

inProgressTasks.title: Junction Client #001 Sprint
inProgressTasks.notes: 2-week sprint continuing, but website changes blocked on git push

blockers.needsFromTom: Is the film uploaded? Is YouTube premiere scheduled?

blockers.impact: Reduces viral reach without tagging strategy
Fake credentials still visible on production site

ideasFromTeam.idea: Monday 'Work From Home' Lunch Hour Launch
ideasFromTeam.rationale: If weekend window fully passes, Monday 11am-1pm is next optimal. People on lunch breaks, high engagement.
Create a system where Tom can pre-authorize launch with a single message ('YouTube is up') that triggers autonomous execution.

ideasFromTeam.idea: Junction Soft Launch Without Git Push
ideasFromTeam.rationale: Can we start outreach without the website? Portfolio + Loom demos might be enough for initial conversations.
After 10+ no-responses on weekend, reduce heartbeat frequency to conserve resources and reduce log noise.

improvements.suggestion: Track patterns - if Tom was out late Friday/Saturday, expect Sunday unavailability. Don't plan launches for morning after big nights.
Allow Tom to pre-authorize launches: 'If X is ready, execute Y without asking'
After 5 consecutive no-responses on weekend, reduce check frequency from 15min to 2h

lessonsLearned.lesson: 63+ checks without human response = protocol failure, not persistence success
lessonsLearned.context: Checking every 15 min for 2 days straight serves no one. Need smarter escalation and frequency adjustment.

lessonsLearned.lesson: Weekend launches are high-risk without T-2 confirmation
lessonsLearned.context: Should have gotten firm commitment from Tom by Thursday evening for Saturday launch.

lessonsLearned.lesson: All systems armed means nothing without human confirmation on blockers
lessonsLearned.context: Perfect marketing assets, zero deployment. Human confirmation is the bottleneck.

lessonsLearned.lesson: Valentine's Day weekend = don't expect availability
lessonsLearned.context: Should have predicted Tom would be occupied Friday-Sunday. Calendar awareness needed.

collaborationRating.notes: Low-activity day. Zero tasks shipped due to human unavailability. Agents maintained readiness but couldn't execute.
highlights: Perfect launch readiness maintained across 63 checks
Detailed logging of all status checks
Team pivoted framing from Valentine's Day to 'Weekend Premiere'
improvements: 63 checks without response = inefficient resource use
No creative work done while waiting - could have advanced other projects
Should have reduced check frequency after ~10 no-responses

tomorrowPriorities.impact: Remove fake credentials from live site

tomorrowPriorities.note: Weekly review was scheduled for today 8pm - may push to Monday if Tom unavailable
Can do research/planning without git push blocker

tonightFocus.critical: If Tom surfaces before 9pm → execute ALTERNATE launch immediately
secondary: Weekly review at 8pm if Tom available
tonightFocus.note: Evening window closes at 9pm. If missed, pivot to Monday lunch hour.
